records.display_name: 125th Street

description: Crosstown bus corridor in Manhattan serving routes Bx15, M60, M100, M101

Corridor in Manhattan where the 125th Street Transportation Improvements Project and proposed M60 Select Bus Service are focused. Key intersections mentioned include Lenox Avenue, Lexington Avenue, Park Avenue, Third Avenue, and Second Avenue.
125th Street corridor in Manhattan, New York City. Bus routes Bx15, M60, M100, M101 operate on this corridor. Over 30,000 bus passengers daily.
125th Street corridor in Manhattan where M60 SBS and local bus routes operate
Major east-west corridor in Manhattan, proposed for Select Bus Service between Morningside Avenue and Second Avenue
Major east-west corridor in Manhattan serving bus routes M60 SBS, Bx15, M100, M101
Major crosstown street in Manhattan serving as the primary corridor for M60 SBS and local bus routes
Street corridor in Manhattan serving the M60 SBS route between the Hudson River and the RFK Bridge
125th Street corridor in Manhattan from river to river, proposed for Select Bus Service improvements
125th Street corridor from Amsterdam Avenue to Second Avenue, including bus lane section between Lenox Avenue and Second Avenue
limits: from Morningside Avenue to Second Avenue
from Amsterdam Av to Second Av
From Amsterdam Avenue to Second Avenue

records.display_name: Bx15 Local

description: Crosstown bus route on 125th Street
Local service on Third Avenue, replaces the Bx55 route (weekday only) with an all times route, local stops from The Hub to Fordham Plaza
Proposed limited-stop service on Third Avenue, replacing the Bx55 LTD. Local stops: Harlem 125th St to The Hub, limited stops: The Hub to Fordham Plaza.
Bx15 Limited will provide limited-stop service between The Hub and Fordham Plaza along Third Avenue and local service between West Harlem and The Hub. Renamed from Bx55 Limited.
Bx15 Local will provide all-stop service between The Hub and Fordham Plaza along Third Avenue.
Bus route on 125th Street.
Bx15 Limited connecting Fordham Plaza in the Bronx with 125th Street in Manhattan, replacing the Bx55
